title: Two GraphTraversal adapter methods are unused prod passthroughs that ignore instance database

findingDetails.evidence: BetterSqliteGraphTraversal.collectWeightedWalk (lines 57-61) and collectDirectedReachability (lines 63-67) delegate straight to the imported module functions and never reference this.database. A grep across non-test/non-dist code finds production calls only to collectCausalWeightedNeighbors (causal-boost.ts:428) and collectDependencyReachability (memo.ts:223); the other two adapter methods have no production caller.
findingDetails.why: Dead interface surface: instance methods that ignore instance state add maintenance weight and falsely imply the adapter owns those traversals, while the only value (db binding) is absent. They are reachable only via the contract test.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either drop collectWeightedWalk/collectDirectedReachability from the GraphTraversal port (callers can use the bfs-traversal module functions directly, as they already do internally), or add a one-line comment documenting that they are reserved/contract-only — mirroring the explicit unadopted-port note already present in lexical-search.ts:58-65.
